title: A Novel Approach to JSON-driven Typst Typesetting

content.text: This is a demonstration of rendering *Typst syntax* directly from a structured JSON file [@Smith2023]. Typst is fast and supports natively powerful formatting.
We define our core principle in the following block equation:
The proof relies on standard induction, as originally detailed by Doe [@Doe2024]. For more details, refer to our references at the end of the document.
We can easily insert standalone paragraphs without needing a heading attached to them.

content.caption: A structural overview of the system showing the data flow. Notice how *Typst markup* works inside captions too!

content.text: Let $x$ be the number of nodes in the network, and let $alpha$ represent the learning rate. As demonstrated in our previous work, the threshold condition $T >= x/2 + pi$ must be satisfied for the system to remain stable.

content.caption: Experimental results comparing our method against the baseline. Values in $"ms"$.

references.citation: B. Smith, *Automating Document Generation*, Journal of Code, 2023.
